title: ABNF, Tokens, Whitespace, Line Endings, Limits, and Parser Architecture · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A protocol pairing room predicts byte, parser, state, ownership, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first strict bounded incremental parser architecture for abnf, tokens, whitespace, line endings, limits, and parser architecture. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of strict bounded incremental parser architecture while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Translate RFC ABNF alternatives, repetition, terminals, lists, and prose constraints into explicit parser states and tests.
Validate tokens and visible bytes using octet classes while rejecting separators, controls, NUL, and forbidden whitespace.
Require CRLF at HTTP/1.1 structural boundaries and document any local robustness exception without parser disagreement.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Abnf Derivation
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop protocol team handles case 9152 in a incident replay lab retaining minimized request-smuggling corpora. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate strict grammar and resource limits before any application effect; preserve this operating constraint: the maintained Go protocol stack remains the production transfer target; then append a second pipelined message and prove the first parser leaves the exact residual bytes. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Translate RFC ABNF alternatives, repetition, terminals, lists, and prose constraints into explicit parser states and tests.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Parse Validate Effect Phases
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Parse Validate Effect Phases: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop protocol team handles case 9161 in a privacy-sensitive proxy translating between two parser implementations. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate strict grammar and resource limits before any application effect; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then replace one field with repeated non-combinable lines and preserve their order. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate byte recognition, structural validation, semantic validation, policy, and effects so failure cannot partially authorize work.
